description: Multi-Role Auth Page & Admin User Management - Customers self-register; login serves all three roles (customer, operator, admin) and routes each to its own area; operators and admins are created by an admin from a new Admin Dashboard with a user-management section. Adds an admin-only API to create and list users, hardens public signup so it can only ever create customers.

userStories.title: Admin-only create-user API
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a secure endpoint to create operator and admin accounts so that elevated accounts are never self-registered.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/admin-routes.js and mount it at /api/admin in src/api.js
POST /api/admin/users is protected by requireAuth then requireRole('admin')
Accepts JSON body { name, email, password, role } and validates: name/email/password required, email matches EMAIL_RE, password length >= 6
role must be one of ['operator','admin']; reject 'customer' and unknown values with 400
Returns 409 when the email already exists (lowercased, like signup)
Password stored only as a bcrypt hash via hashPassword; response never includes passwordHash
On success returns 201 with public user { id, name, email, role }
Non-admin token returns 403; missing/invalid token returns 401
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added src/admin-routes.js (POST /api/admin/users), mounted at /api/admin in src/api.js. requireAuth + requireRole('admin'); validates name/email/password/role; role restricted to operator|admin; 409 on duplicate (lowercased) email; bcrypt hash via hashPassword; 201 returns public user. Tested DB-free in test/admin-routes.test.js via in-memory db fake + real signed tokens.

userStories.title: Admin-only list-users API
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to list existing users so the dashboard can show who already has access.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/users protected by requireAuth + requireRole('admin')
Returns { users: [{ id, name, email, role, createdAt }, ...] } ordered by createdAt (or id) descending
Response never includes passwordHash
Optional ?role=operator|admin|customer filters by role when present; invalid values are ignored
Non-admin returns 403; unauthenticated returns 401
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added GET /api/admin/users to src/admin-routes.js (requireAuth + requireRole('admin')). Returns { users: [{id,name,email,role,createdAt}] } ordered by createdAt DESC, id DESC. Optional ?role=operator|admin|customer filter via FILTERABLE_ROLES (customer allowed here, unlike create); invalid/absent role returns all. SELECT lists explicit columns so passwordHash is never selected. Added 5 tests (401/403 guards, newest-first + no passwordHash, role filter, invalid-role-returns-all). Full suite: 34 pass. Typecheck clean.

userStories.title: Backend signup hardening (defense in depth)
userStories.description: As a security-conscious developer, I want public signup to be incapable of creating elevated accounts even if the client sends a role.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/signup ignores any role field in the body and always inserts role = 'customer'
A test asserts posting { role: 'admin' } to /api/auth/signup still yields a customer account
Existing test/auth.test.js continues to pass
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Signup route already destructured only {name,email,password} and hard-coded role='customer' on INSERT, so it ignores any client-supplied role by construction. Added test/auth-routes.test.js (3 tests via the require.cache db-fake pattern): customer signup happy path, posting {role:'admin'} still yields a customer (checks both response and stored row), and {role:'operator'} likewise. No production code change required. Full suite: 37 pass. Typecheck clean.

userStories.title: Admin Dashboard view shell + role routing
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to land on a dedicated dashboard after login so I have a home base instead of the customer booking view.
acceptanceCriteria: Add <div id='admin-view' class='view'> to app.html, structured like operator-view (nav with logo, role badge 'Admin Console', avatar wired to logout())
routeForRole() gains an admin case that calls showView('admin-view'), sets the avatar initial + welcome line, and triggers loading the user list
Admins no longer fall back to the booking view
Customer and operator routing are unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: logging in as admin@irago.com / iragoadmin@123 lands on the Admin Dashboard
userStories.notes: Added <div id='admin-view' class='view'> in app.html mirroring operator-view (booking-nav with logo, 'Admin Console' purple badge via new .admin-role-badge, #admin-avatar wired to logout()). Body uses existing op-body/op-welcome/op-section-title classes; #admin-users container holds the user list (US-005). routeForRole() gained an 'admin' case: sets #admin-avatar initial + #admin-welcome line, showView('admin-view'), calls loadAdminUsers(). Added a placeholder loadAdminUsers() (US-005 fleshes it out). Admins no longer fall back to booking; customer/operator routing unchanged. Typecheck + 37 tests pass. Browser verification NOT done (no dev-browser/MCP tooling in this env) — needs manual check that admin@irago.com lands on the Admin Dashboard.

userStories.title: User list on the Admin Dashboard
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see existing users so I know who already has access and what role they hold.
acceptanceCriteria: On entering the Admin Dashboard, the client calls GET /api/admin/users via the shared authed fetch wrapper and renders a list/table with name, email, and a role badge
Role badges are visually distinct (admin / operator / customer colors), reusing existing badge styling where possible
Loading, empty, and error states are handled (e.g. 'Could not load users')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: list shows the seeded admin, operators, and customers

userStories.title: Add-user form on the Admin Dashboard
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a form to add an operator or another admin so I can provision team accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Dashboard has an 'Add Team Member' form with fields Full Name, Email, Password, and a Role selector limited to Operator and Admin
Submitting calls POST /api/admin/users using the shared authed fetch wrapper (sends bearer token)
Client-side validation mirrors the server (all fields required, password >= 6) with inline errors using existing auth-error styling (showAuthError/hideAuthError)
On success the form clears, a success message shows, and the user list refreshes to include the new account
Server errors (409 duplicate email, 403) are surfaced as readable messages
Submit button shows a busy state while the request is in flight (reuse setBusy)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: add an operator, confirm it appears in the list and can then log in

userStories.title: Auth page clarity - customer-only registration, all-role login
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the auth page to make clear that registration is for customers while operators/admins sign in with provisioned accounts, so I don't get confused.
acceptanceCriteria: The registration card is explicitly framed as customer signup (heading/subtext e.g. 'Create your customer account')
A short helper line tells operators/admins to sign in with the account provided by their administrator (no public operator/admin registration)
The login card heading/subtext communicates it serves all roles (single shared login)
No role selector is added to the public signup form
Existing customer signup and login flows still work unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: copy renders correctly and toggling between login/register still works
